metadata.title: Recommended Practice (RP-018) Response to Data Inc/Dec Controllers
metadata.summary: MMA/AMEI Recommended Practice RP-018: Recommended Practice (RP-018) Response to Data Inc/Dec Controllers.

problems.description: The value used by the Data Increment or Data Decreme. t controller is not specified.
Does the increment/decrement affect the MSB, the LSB, or the full 14 bit data value of the parameter? It seems obvious

recommendation: Under this recommendation, the value byte for both messages is ignored ("don't care"). When applied to Registered Parameter numbers 0, and 1, Data Increment and Data Decrement messages will increase or decrease the LSB by 1 (currently unspecified in MIDI 1.0). When applied to Registered Parameter numbers 2, 3, and 4, Data Increment and Data Decrement messages will increase or decrease the MSB by 1 (as specified in the 4.2 Addendum to MIDI 1.0, page 20). For future Registered Parameters, behavior of Data Increment and Data Decrement must be specified; if it is not, reception of these messages will increase or decrease the LSB by 1. For Non-Registered Parameters, behavior of Data Increment and Data Decrement must be specified by the manufacturer for each parameter.
rpn_behavior: When applied to Registered Parameter numbers 0, and 1, Data Increment and Data Decrement messages will increase or decrease the LSB by 1 (currently unspecified in MIDI 1.0). When applied to Registered Parameter numbers 2, 3, and 4, Data Increment and Data Decrement messages will increase or decrease the MSB by 1 (as specified in the 4.2 Addendum to MIDI 1.0, page 20).
For future Registered Parameters, behavior of Data Increment and Data Decrement must be specified; if it is not, reception of these messages will increase or decrease the LSB by 1.
nrpn_behavior: For Non-Registered Parameters, behavior of Data Increment and Data Decrement must be specified by the manufacturer for each parameter.
receiving_device_rules: Receiving devices should keep track of the current value of a parameter and should increment the LSB by 1 when Data
Increment is received, or decrement the LSB by 1 when Data Decrement is received. If the LSB is not supported by that
device, then the parameter change should be made when the LSB wraps into the MSB, or as defined by the parameter. For
example, a device that does not support the LSB (cents) value for Pitch Bend Sensitivity (RPN #0) should increment the MSB
(semitones) value when the LSB is incremented to 100, at which time the LSB should be reset to 0.

example.description: For example, to increment pitch bend sensitivity on MIDI channel 1 by 2 cents (all numbers are in hexadecimal):

steps.raw: $B0 $65 $7F set Registered Parameter Number MSB to NULL
steps.description: set Registered Parameter Number MSB to NULL

steps.raw: $B0 $64 $7F set Registered Parameter Number LSB to NULL
steps.description: set Registered Parameter Number LSB to NULL
